info.title: Enterprise Data Federation Service (EDFS)
info.description:
Enterprise Data Federation Service (EDFS or EDF) is a set of iTwin integration services designed to enable integration of Bentley iTwin platform solutions with any external enterprise system or user solutions (such as SAP®, IBM® Maximo™, Microsoft® SharePoint, ESRI, etc.). EDFS is the latest generation (6th) of BECS integration engine.
EDFS is designed to support any requested integration by running any BECS integration package from Bentley Integration Catalogue (BIC). Thanks to this flexibility and zero coding needed for design or maintenance of integration packages is easy to adapt any integration request.
Main mission of EDFS is to provide zero coding, ready to use, Bentley catalogue based integration gateway to any user enterprise system or solution.

get.description:
---

Retrieve a list of currently subscribed integration packages by organization.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `edfs_projmng` permission assigned at the Organizational level or be an Organization Administrator.

---

packages.name: Sql Customers
packages.description: A package for retrieving data from an SQL Database table

packages.name: SAP Work Orders
packages.description: A package for retrieving Work Orders from SAP

patch.description:
---

Enable or disable integration package timer.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `edfs_listmng` permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

put.summary: Remove selected package role to iTwin project role to assignments
put.description:
---

Remove only the specified integration package role to iTwin project role assignments for the integration package.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given Project or have `administration_manage_roles` permission assigned at the Project level.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

User must also have these permissions assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.
- `edfs_ilsmng` to Manage Access to specific Integration Packages
	
Each integration package role has its own permission(s) assigned and the user who wants to manage them must have these permissions assigned as well.

__Package roles and associated permissions__
- Execute Integration Package
	- `edfs_objipexec` to Execute Integration Package(s)

---

parameters.name: uniqueName
parameters.description: The unique name of integration package

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

403.description: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

error.message: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

404.description: This response indicates iTwin or integration package was not found.

error.message: Requested AssignmentList is not available.

422.description: The 422 (Unprocessable Entity) status code indicates that the request cannot be processed by the server due to a client error (e.g. malformed request syntax)

error.message: Cannot update AssignmentList.

details.message: Provided iTwin Role ID value is not valid.
Provided Package Role ID value is not valid.
Provided iTwin ID value is not valid.
Provided Unique Name value contains invalid characters.

429.description: This response indicates that the client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

patch.summary: Patch package role to iTwin project role assignments
patch.description:
---

Patch existing integration package role to iTwin project role to assignments for the integration package.

You can add integration package role to new iTwin project role
- By [Adding package role to iTwin project role assignments](/apis/edfs/operations/packages-roles-assignments-add/).

You can retrieve the available packageRoleIds
- By [getting integration package roles](/apis/edfs/operations/packages-roles-get/)

You can retrieve the available iTwinRoleIds for your iTwin project
- By [getting iTwin roles](/apis/access-control-v2/operations/get-iTwin-roles/)

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given Project or have `administration_manage_roles` permission assigned at the Project level.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

User must also have the following permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin:
- `edfs_ilsmng` to Manage Access to specific Integration Packages
	
Each integration package role has its own permission(s) assigned and the user who wants to manage them must have these permissions assigned as well.

__Package roles and associated permissions__
- Execute Integration Package
	- `edfs_objipexec` to Execute Integration Package(s)

---

post.summary: Add package role to iTwin project role assignments
post.description:
---

Adds new integration package role to iTwin project role assignments for the integration package.

You can retrieve the available packageRoleIds
- By [getting integration package roles](/apis/edfs/operations/packages-roles-get/)

You can retrieve the available iTwinRoleIds for your iTwin project
- By [getting iTwin roles](/apis/access-control-v2/operations/get-iTwin-roles/)

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given Project or have `administration_manage_roles` permission assigned at the Project level.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. User must also have the following permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin:
- `edfs_ilsmng` to Manage Access to specific Integration Packages
	
Each integration package role has its own permission(s) assigned and the user who wants to manage them must have these permissions assigned as well.

__Package roles and associated permissions__
- Execute Integration Package
	- `edfs_objipexec` to Execute Integration Package(s)

post.description:
---

Execute the specified integration package for the specified iTwin. Other integration package specific variables can be provided in the body.

To get an example of input for each integration package you can retrieve the details
- By [getting details of subscribed integration package](/apis/edfs/operations/subscribed-packages-details-get/)

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the EDFS `Execute Integration Package` role assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.
Package role assignments can be set
- By [setting integration package roles](/apis/edfs/operations/packages-roles-assignments-add/)

---

parameters.description: The unique name of integration package

get.description:
---

Retrieve external system type and external system type credential properties for the integration package.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `edfs_credmng` permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

---

parameters.name: uniqueName
parameters.description: The unique name of integration package

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

404.description: This response indicates iTwin, integration package or system type was not found.

error.message: Requested PropertyList is not available.

422.description: The 422 (Unprocessable Entity) status code indicates that the request cannot be processed by the server due to a client error (e.g. malformed request syntax)

error.message: Cannot retrieve SystemPropertyList.

details.message: Provided iTwin ID value is not valid.
Provided Unique Name value contains invalid characters.
Provided System Type value contains invalid characters.

429.description: This response indicates that the client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

post.description:
---

Create external system information and its credentials set.

You need to provide the `SystemId` (unique within the integration package) and the `SystemType`. You can retrieve them
- By [getting integration package system types](/apis/edfs/operations/system-types-get/)

You also need to provide the `credentials`. These are system properties (different for each system type). You can retrieve them
- By [getting integration package system type properties](/apis/edfs/operations/system-type-properties-get/)

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `edfs_credmng` permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

---

401.description: This response indicates that request lacks valid authentication credentials. Access token might not been provided, issued by the wrong issuer, does not have required scopes or request headers were malformed.

error.message: Header Authorization was not found in the request. Access denied.

403.description: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

error.message: The user has insufficient permissions for the requested operation.

404.description: This response indicates that the requested operation or resource could not be found.

error.message: The requested resource was not found. Verify the API URL and the Accept header.

details.message: Unable to match incoming request to an operation.

409.description: This response indicates that the user tried to create a resource that already exists.

error.message: A system with the same parameters already exists.

details.message: A system with the specified SystemId already exists.

422.description: The 422 (Unprocessable Entity) status code indicates that the request cannot be processed by the server due to a client error (e.g. malformed request syntax)

error.message: Cannot create System.

details.message: Provided System ID value contains invalid characters.
Required property Name is missing.
Provided System Type value exceeds maximum length 150.
Provided Credentials value contains invalid characters.
Provided iTwin ID value is not valid.

429.description: This response indicates that the client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

retry-after.description: Number of seconds to wait until client is allowed to make more requests.

error.message: The client sent more requests than allowed by this API for the current tier of the client.

get.description:
---

Retrieve information about the currently available integration package roles assignable to iTwin roles for the integration package.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given Project or have `administration_manage_roles` permission assigned at the Project level.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

User must also have the following permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin:
- `edfs_ilsmng` to Manage Access to specific Integration Packages

---

post.description:
---

Insert integration package timer.

You need to provide the `packageUniqueName` which is the uniqueName of the integration package the Organization that owns a given iTwin is subscribed to.
You need to provide the `cronStr` that represents the cron expression (a cron expression is a string consisting of six or seven subexpressions/fields that define a schedule). The integration package will be executed according to this schedule.
You need to provide the `parameters`. Parameters are input variables for the integration package. To get an example of the input for each integration package you can retrieve the details
- By [getting details of subscribed integration package](/apis/edfs/operations/subscribed-packages-details-get/)

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must have the `edfs_listmng` permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin.

---

example.name: SAP_WORK_ORDERS at 00:00 on Saturday and Sunday
SAP_WORK_ORDERS at 00:00 on Saturday and Sunday

put.summary: Remove all package role assignments for specified iTwin roles
put.description:
---

Remove all existing integration package role assignments for specified iTwin roles for the integration package.

### Authentication

Requires `Authorization` header with valid Bearer token for scope `itwin-platform`.

For more documentation on authorization and how to get access token visit [OAUTH2 Authorization](https://developer.bentley.com/apis/overview/authorization/) page.

### Authorization

User must be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given Project or have `administration_manage_roles` permission assigned at the Project level.

An Organization Administrator must have at least one of the following roles assigned in User Management: Account Administrator, Co-Administrator, or CONNECT Services Administrator. For more information about User Management please visit our Bentley Communities [Licensing, Cloud, and Web Services](https://bentleysystems.service-now.com/community?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=1e5410491b7d8a90f3fc5287624bcb57) wiki page.

User must also have the following permission assigned at the iTwin level or be an Organization Administrator for the Organization that owns a given iTwin:
- `edfs_ilsmng` to Manage Access to specific Integration Packages
	
Each integration package role has its own permission(s) assigned and the user who wants to manage them must have these permissions assigned as well.

__Package roles and associated permissions__
- Execute Integration Package
	- `edfs_objipexec` to Execute Integration Package(s)

---
